person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Biography
Uncertain Origin/Parentage
Avis, born about 1617, based on her 1635 marriage, is of uncertain origin. 

Avis is described as "of Knole, County Somerset, England" in The Granberry Family and Allied Families ..., by Jacobus. Other than this, very little else is known about her early life. Current genealogists agree that her maiden name was Chapman (or "Chepman" in marriage record). However, some older works reported her maiden name as Deacon.

Avis Deacon as the name for the wife of William Reed originated with J. L. Reed's, Reed Genealogy Descendants of William Reade, of Weymouth, Mass., from 1635-1902 (1901), and was repeated in Reed Descendants: Thirteen Generations ... (1955) by Aldridge. J. L. Reed attributes "family legend" for his information. The association was made when it was found that Avis Deacon immigrated on the "Alice" in 1635, and her twin brother, Thomas, came over on the "Assurance" "with William Reed." This is an obvious case of mistaking one William Reed/Read for another. In fact, the Reads who were aboard the Assurance were expressly on board "to be transported to Virginea," and so was Avis Deacon! The 1635 voyage of the Alice also went to Virginia.

Marriage
Avis Chapman or Chepman married 26 October 1635 at Long Sutton, Somersetshire, England William Reed.

Caution, Multiple William Read/Reeds. 
There were two William Reed/Reads in early Weymouth. They have been badly mixed by many authors, and this confusion has also found its way online. Please see the profile for Avis' husband, William Reed, for further information. The other William Read married Susan Hayme and Ruth Crooke.

Robert C. Anderson, in his Great Migration series, warns that there were multiple William Reads living in Boston and Weymouth at the same time, and that their biographies are so confused that Anderson chose not to deal with the problems. He warned that the works of Pope and Savage "muddled the records." The one work he recommends Jacobus', The Granberry Family and Allied Families, Including the Ancestry of Helen (Woodward) Granberry. He called Weymouth History by George Walter Chamberlain "less successful, but still useful," and Jacob Whittemore Reed's History of the Reed Family in Europe and America, (Boston 1861) "worthless."

Children:
1.) Margaret Reed bpt 20 Jan 1635/6 Long Sutton; mar 11 May 1657 to John Vining

2.) Hannah Reed b 1637; mar 2 Dec 1658 Weymouth to Nicholas Whitmarsh

3.) Ruth Reed b 1642 (Aldridge, p13), d 17 Aug 1663 Weymouth; mar 19 Dec 1662 John Whitman

4.) Thomas Reed b c. 1645 Weymouth

5.) Mary Reed b 1647 Weymouth, d 16 April 1655

6.) John Reed d 13 Jan 1720/2 Dighton, MA; mar Bethia Frye, daughter of George Fry/Frye of Taunton

7.) William Reed b bef 1650 prob at Long Sutton, Somerset, England

8.) James Reed b betw 1657-60 Weymouth

Note: 
The William Read born 15 December 1649 is attributed by Anderson to the other William Read of Weymouth.

Immigration
Avis and her family immigrated some time before William was made a freeman at Weymouth on 18 May 1653. It is usually said that the family probably arrived at Weymouth about 1650.

Weymouth
Avis continued to live at Weymouth after William had died. On 6 February, 1659/60, the widow Avis Reed was granted a swamp lot.

The widow Avis was mentioned in a record of 06 April 1658: "to have liberty to take in a garden plott before her house." On 06 February 1659/60, "it was further granted that Widow Avis Read should have a swamp lott by virtue that her Husband was then an Inhabitant when the sayd Swamp lotts were granted." On 14 December 1663, widow Read was granted 10 acres in the First Division and 30 acres in the Second Division. Also, "land of Widdow Avis Read" was mentioned in a deed from John Whitman to John Vining dated 19 December 1670. This is the last record of her.

Death and Legacy
Avis died after 1670 at Weymouth, Suffolk (later Norfolk) County, Massachusetts. 

William died between 11 January 1657/8 and 6 April 1658, at Weymouth, Suffolk (later Norfolk) County, Massachusetts. On the January date, William purchased some lumber in Weymouth, and on the April date, the town approved the widow Avis Reed's request to start a front-yard garden.
___________________________________________________
U.S. President [COOLIDGE]'s 8-Great Grandmother.       `Osawatomie' Brown's 6-Great Grandmother

zh.lifeSummary: 當Avis Chapman於1617年出生在英國薩默塞特朗 塞頓時，她的父親Thomas Chepman50歲，母親Margaret Derby47歲。她於1635年10月8日與William Reede Sr.在英國薩默塞特朗 塞頓結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她於1635年住在英國薩默塞特可諾爾。她去世於1676年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可維姆斯享年59歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可維姆斯諾斯 維姆斯 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Avis Chapman于1617年出生在英国薩默塞特朗 塞頓时，她的父亲Thomas Chepman50岁，母亲Margaret Derby47岁。她于1635年10月8日与William Reede Sr.在英国薩默塞特朗 塞頓结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她于1635年住在英国薩默塞特可諾爾。她去世于1676年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可維姆斯享年59岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可維姆斯諾斯 維姆斯 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Chapman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a merchant or trader, Middle English chap(pe)man, chepman, Old English cēapmann, cēpemann, a compound of cēap ‘barter, bargain, price, property’ + mann ‘man’.
Jewish: adopted probably for a like-sounding or like-meaning name in some other European language; see for example   Kaufman  .

History: This name was brought independently to North America from England by numerous different bearers from the 17th century onward. John Chapmen (sic) was one of the free planters who assented to the ‘Fundamental Agreement’ of the New Haven Colony on June 4, 1639.

variants.name: Kaufman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Meyer, Chaim, Emanuel, Moshe, Eytan, Herschel, Zvi, Ari, Aron.
Americanized form of German   Kaufmann  , and a variant of the same Jewish surname.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the personal name Kaufman meaning ‘merchant’. Compare Koyfman 2.

variants.name: Chipman
meanings: English: from Middle English chipman (Old English cȳp(e)mann), a side form of chapman, chepman ‘merchant, trader’; see Chapman , with which this name was interchangeable.

variants.name: Copeman
meanings: English (Norfolk and Suffolk): patronymic from the early Middle English personal name Cop(e)man, an Anglicized form of Old Scandinavian Kaupmaðr. It is a transferred use of Old Scandinavian kaupmaðr (Old Danish køpman) ‘merchant’, cognate with Old English cēapmann (see Chapman ), but there is no evidence for the occupational term in Middle English.
Americanized form of North German   Koopmann   or Dutch   Koopman  .

nameMeanings.name: Avis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French form of the ancient Germanic name Aveza, derived from a short form of one or more compound names containing the first element av (as in Ava ; the meaning is uncertain). The correspondence in form to the Latin word avis ‘bird’ is coincidental.

variants.name: Avice
meanings: From a Norman French form of the ancient Germanic name Aveza, derived from a short form of one or more compound names containing the first element av (as in Ava ; the meaning is uncertain). The correspondence in form to the Latin word avis ‘bird’ is coincidental.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Avis Chapman was born in 1617, in Long Sutton, Somerset, England, her father, Thomas Chepman, was 50 and her mother, Margaret Derby, was 47. She married William Reede Sr. on 8 October 1635, in Long Sutton, Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. She lived in Knole, Somerset, England in 1635. She died in 1676, in Weymouth, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 59, and was buried in North Weymouth Cemetery, Weymouth, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Avis Chapman est née en 1617, à Long Sutton, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Chepman, avait 50 ans et sa mère, Margaret Derby, avait 47 ans. Elle a épousé William Reede Sr. le 8 octobre 1635, à Long Sutton, Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Elle a vécu à Knole, Somerset, Angleterre en 1635. Elle est décédée en 1676, à Weymouth, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 59 ans, et a été enterrée à North Weymouth Cemetery, Weymouth, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

ja.lifeSummary: Avis Chapmanが1617年にイギリスソメルセトロング スットオンで生まれた時，父Thomas Chepmanは50歳，母Margaret Derbyは47歳であった。1635年10月8日にイギリスソメルセトロング スットオンでWilliam Reede Sr.と結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1635年にはイギリスソメルセトノルに住んでいた。彼女は1676年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクウエイモウスで59歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクウエイモウスノース ウエイモウス セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Avis Chapman(이)가 1617년 영국 서머세트 론그 수턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Chepman(은)는 50세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Derby(은)는 47세였다. 그녀는 1635년 10월 8일 영국 서머세트 론그 수턴에서 William Reede Sr.(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 1635년도에 영국 서머세트 노일에서 살았다. 그녀는 1676년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 베이모우스에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 베이모우스 노르스 베이모우스 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Avis Chapman nasceu em 1617, em Long Sutton, Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Chepman, tinha 50 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Derby, tinha 47 anos. Ela casou-se com William Reede Sr. em 8 de outubro de 1635, em Long Sutton, Somerset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela viveu em Knole, Somerset, Inglaterra em 1635. Ela faleceu em 1676, em Weymouth, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 59 anos, e foi sepultada em North Weymouth Cemetery, Weymouth, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Avis Chapman родилась в 1617 г. в местности лонг суттон, сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Chepman было 50 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Derby, 47. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Reede Sr. 8 октября 1635 г. в местности лонг суттон, сомирсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Она жила в местности нол, сомирсет, Англия в 1635 году. Она умерла в 1676 г. в местности вимоут, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 59 лет и была похоронена в местности норт вимоут кеметири, вимоут, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.
